items.text: Each team consists of one to three *active quizzers* at the table. Additional quizzers sit behind the table as *inactive quizzers*.
The *head coach* designates a *captain* for each match.
Active quizzers may only have closed *Scripture Portions* on the table.
Each match consists of twenty questions: eight 10-point, nine 20-point, and three 30-point questions.
After "Question" is called, hands must be above the table, palms down, in contact with the table, pad, or other hand.
Do NOT *buzz in* during *opening remarks* (question number, point value, *Introductory Remarks*) — this is a foul.
Quizzers have 5 seconds to *buzz in* after the question is fully read.
Do NOT begin answering before being *identified* — this is a foul.
You have 30 seconds to complete the question (if *interrupted*) and give your answer after being *identified*.
A question is *interrupted* when you *buzz in* before the last word of the question is spoken.
If you interrupt, you must correctly complete the question AND give the answer.
Correct answer = full point value. Incorrect answer = lose half the point value.
An *interrupted* question answered incorrectly is *reread* to the opposing team.

subsections.title: Quiz Out / Strike Out / Foul Out

items.text: **Quiz Out**: Answer 5 questions correctly → 20 bonus points, must leave table.
**Strike Out**: Answer 3 questions incorrectly → must leave table.
**Foul Out**: Receive 3 individual fouls → must leave table.
Any quizzer who leaves the table can be immediately replaced by an *inactive* *eligible quizzer* without using a *time-out*.
No *verbal communication* among *active quizzers* or between active quizzers and team *members* except during conferring, contesting, responding, and *time-outs*.
Coaches and *inactive quizzers* may non-verbally communicate at any time among themselves.
No communication between team *members* and the audience at any time.
Each team gets 3 time-outs per match (30 seconds each).
Any team *member* may request a *time-out* between questions.
All *members* of both teams may verbally communicate during a *time-out*.
Quizzers may be substituted during a *time-out*.
Only the quizzer whose answer was ruled incorrect may request to confer.
30 seconds to confer with *active quizzers* and *head coach* at the table.
The *head coach* may bring materials to the table during the confer.
Any team *member* may initiate a contest.
Contest on three issues only: (a) question validity, (b) voiding, (c) correctness of the answer.
Each team gets a maximum of 3 *unsuccessful* contests per match.
2 minutes 30 seconds to prepare and present (30-second prep + 2-minute presentation).
Opposing team gets 1 minute to respond after the contest.
A team may withdraw a contest within the first 30 seconds in exchange for a *time-out* (if available).
Judges vote with coins: heads = accept, tails = deny. Majority wins.
Each foul = loss of 5 points.
Buzzing in during *opening remarks*.
Answering before being *identified*.
Answering when another quizzer was *identified*.
Giving *help* to a teammate who *buzzed in*.
Improper communication by an *active quizzer*.
Having open *Scripture Portion* or materials when not allowed.
Flagrant distraction or signaling teammates.
Un-Christlike or unsportsmanlike attitude.
Improper communication by a coach or *inactive quizzer*.
Not stopping at the calling of "time" during a contest.
Coach or *inactive quizzer* gives *help* to an *active quizzer*.
Contesting with intent of using it solely as a *time-out*.
Un-Christlike or unsportsmanlike attitude by a coach or *inactive quizzer*.
If tied after 20 questions, sudden death overtime begins.
One-minute *time-out* is declared before overtime.
10-point substitute questions are read until the tie is broken.
**Standard Question**: Requires an answer from the material being studied.
**Statement and Question**: A statement gives additional information before the question. Both are part of the question and can be *interrupted*.
**Multi-Part Question**: Two or more distinct questions requiring separate answers.
**Scripture Text Question**: Contains "quote" followed by Scripture. Answer must come from the *three-verse context*.
**Application Question**: Requires answers about the author, history, doctrine, or related topics not necessarily found in the material.
**Quotation Question**: Requires a *perfect quotation* from a complete verse or verses.
**Quotation Completion Question**: Requires completing a *perfect quotation*. Cannot be asked after question 17 or in overtime.
**Essence Question**: Requires a verse or verses to be given in essence (own words with same meaning). Does not have to be a *perfect quotation*.
**Essence Completion Question**: Requires completing a verse or verses in essence.
